items.text: Where did the pain start, and has it moved anywhere?
choices: Periumbilical pain migrating to the right lower quadrant
Right upper quadrant pain, worse after fatty food
Epigastric pain radiating to the back

items.text: How suddenly did the pain start, and how bad is it compared to any pain you've had before?
choices: Gradual onset, mild to moderate severity
Sudden onset, worst pain of the patient's life
Pain out of proportion to the physical findings

items.text: Did you vomit before the pain started, or after?
choices: Vomiting began after the pain started

items.text: Are you able to pass gas or have a bowel movement?
Have you noticed black, tarry stools, red blood in your stool, or vomited blood?
Do you have an irregular heartbeat, a clotting disorder, or a known aneurysm in your belly?
choices: Known atrial fibrillation or hypercoagulable state

items.text: Do you drink heavily, have gallstones, or have high triglycerides?
choices: Heavy or binge alcohol use preceding symptom onset

items.text: Is there any chance you could be pregnant, and have you had any vaginal bleeding or sudden one-sided pelvic pain?
choices: Not applicable or none of these
Sudden severe unilateral pelvic pain with nausea

items.text: Do you have any burning with urination, or pain in your side that spreads toward your groin?
choices: Colicky flank pain radiating to the groin with hematuria

items.text: Do you have a hernia that has become painful, bulging, or that you can't push back in?
choices: Known hernia now painful, bulging, or irreducible

items.text: Have you had abdominal surgery in the past?
Did you have sudden pain in one testicle along with nausea or vomiting?
choices: Sudden unilateral testicular pain with nausea or vomiting

items.text: Do you have inflammatory bowel disease, and does this feel like a prior flare, or have you had bloody diarrhea?
choices: No known inflammatory bowel disease or bloody diarrhea
Known inflammatory bowel disease, pain resembles a prior flare

items.text: Do you have diabetes, and have you noticed excessive thirst, urination, or a fruity breath smell?
choices: Known diabetes with polyuria or polydipsia
Known diabetes with fruity breath odor or rapid, deep breathing

items.text: Do you regularly take NSAIDs like ibuprofen, or a blood thinner?
Do you get a squeezing pain in your upper belly with exertion, or have you been diagnosed with heart disease?
choices: No known coronary disease or exertional discomfort
Known coronary artery disease without exertional epigastric symptoms

items.text: Assess general appearance and vital signs.
Palpate the abdomen for tenderness, guarding, and rebound.
Assess McBurney point tenderness and Rovsing, psoas, or obturator signs.
choices: Positive Rovsing, psoas, or obturator sign

items.text: Assess the right upper quadrant for a Murphy sign or palpable mass.
Inspect for distention and auscultate bowel sounds.
choices: Distended with high-pitched or hyperactive bowel sounds

items.text: Palpate the abdomen for a pulsatile mass.
Assess for costovertebral-angle tenderness.
Examine hernia sites for a bulge and reducibility.
Perform a pelvic or testicular examination when clinically indicated.
choices: Normal or deferred as not indicated
Cervical motion tenderness or adnexal mass or tenderness
High-riding, tender testicle with absent cremasteric reflex

items.text: Perform a rectal examination when clinically indicated.
choices: Normal, guaiac negative, or deferred as not indicated
Gross blood or melena on examination

items.text: Inspect skin and sclerae for jaundice or flank/periumbilical ecchymosis.
